content: read reviews/578.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/578.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/578.md.

    24‚Üí- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos578`.

    27‚Üí## 5. Custom Definitions
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí- [x] `hypercubeGraph` has a LaTeX docstring describing the d-dimensional hypercube graph.
    30‚Üí- [x] `toGraph578` has a LaTeX docstring explaining the Boolean-matrix graph construction and its connection to G(n, 1/2).
    31‚Üí- [x] Definitions are computable (Finset operations on finite types); `noncomputable` not needed.
    32‚Üí- [x] No `sorry` in definitions ‚ÄĒ `symm` and `loopless` fields are fully proved in both definitions.
    33‚Üí- [x] Function names use `lowerCamelCase`: `hypercubeGraph`, `toGraph578`.
    34‚Üí- [x] No local notation needed or used.
    35‚Üí- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks on definitions. (Optional ‚ÄĒ "consider adding" per checklist.)
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí## 6. Theorem Statements
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
    40‚Üí- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_578` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
    41‚Üí- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.
    42‚Üí- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5 60]`.
    43‚Üí- [x] Main theorem has a docstring describing the probabilistic statement and its solution.
    44‚Üí- [x] Docstring references source papers: `[Er90c]`, `[Ri00]`.
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí### `answer()` usage
    47‚Üí
    48‚ÜíThe problem is a direct assertion: "the probability that G(2^d, 1/2) contains Q_d tends to 1 as d ‚Üí ‚ąě." This is **Case 3** (direct assertion). The formalization correctly states the claim directly without an `answer()` wrapper.
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly (no `answer()` wrapper) ‚ÄĒ correct for Case 3.
    51‚Üí- [x] All quantifiers (`‚ąÄ őĶ`, `‚ąÉ d‚āÄ`, `‚ąÄ d`) are in the theorem type, not as theorem parameters.
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí## 7. Category Attribute

    56‚Üí- [x] `research solved` is a valid category value.

    58‚Üí
    59‚Üí## 8. AMS Attribute
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üí- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5 60`.

    72‚Üí- [x] No `native_decide`.
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üí## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí- N/A ‚ÄĒ no variants present. The file contains a single main theorem.
    77‚Üí

    79‚Üí
    80‚Üí- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    81‚Üí- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `‚ąÄ`, `‚ąÉ`, `‚Č•`, `őĶ`, `‚ĄĚ`, `‚Üí`, `‚Č†`, `‚ąß`.
    82‚Üí- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).

    84‚Üí- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
    85‚Üí- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    86‚Üí

    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí- [ ] `lake build` ‚ÄĒ not verified in this review (requires build environment).
    90‚Üí- [ ] Custom linters ‚ÄĒ not verified in this review (requires build environment).
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üí## Summary
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí**PASS.** The file is well-structured and conforms to the checklist. The formalization models the ErdŇĎs‚ÄďBollob√°s conjecture (problem 578) by expressing the probabilistic containment of the hypercube Q_d in G(2^d, 1/2) as a counting statement over Boolean matrices. Two helper definitions (`hypercubeGraph`, `toGraph578`) are cleanly written with full proofs for their `SimpleGraph` fields and proper docstrings. The main theorem correctly uses Case 3 (direct assertion) without `answer()`, and is appropriately categorized as `research solved`. No issues found.
    95‚Üí

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/578.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdŇĎs Problem 578`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/578`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (ErdŇĎs‚ÄďBollob√°s conjecture on hypercube containment in random graphs).
- [x] Bibliography entries included: `[Er90c]` (ErdŇĎs 1990), `[Ri00]` (Riordan 2000).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos578 ... end Erdos578`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos578`.
- [x] `open SimpleGraph` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `hypercubeGraph` has a LaTeX docstring describing the d-dimensional hypercube graph.
- [x] `toGraph578` has a LaTeX docstring explaining the Boolean-matrix graph construction and its connection to G(n, 1/2).
- [x] Definitions are computable (Finset operations on finite types); `noncomputable` not needed.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions ‚ÄĒ `symm` and `loopless` fields are fully proved in both definitions.
- [x] Function names use `lowerCamelCase`: `hypercubeGraph`, `toGraph578`.
- [x] No local notation needed or used.
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks on definitions. (Optional ‚ÄĒ "consider adding" per checklist.)

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_578` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5 60]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring describing the probabilistic statement and its solution.
- [x] Docstring references source papers: `[Er90c]`, `[Ri00]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem is a direct assertion: "the probability that G(2^d, 1/2) contains Q_d tends to 1 as d ‚Üí ‚ąě." This is **Case 3** (direct assertion). The formalization correctly states the claim directly without an `answer()` wrapper.

- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly (no `answer()` wrapper) ‚ÄĒ correct for Case 3.
- [x] All quantifiers (`‚ąÄ őĶ`, `‚ąÉ d‚āÄ`, `‚ąÄ d`) are in the theorem type, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]`: `category research solved`.
- [x] `research solved` is a valid category value.
- [x] Consistent with the problem status: solved by Riordan [Ri00] but proof uses `sorry` (not formally verified).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5 60`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 5, 60).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order: 5 < 60.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both are valid AMS MSC2020 codes (5 = Combinatorics, 60 = Probability theory and stochastic processes).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` (multi-line proof with sorry, acceptable format).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or other non-theorem declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the solved problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A ‚ÄĒ no variants present. The file contains a single main theorem.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `‚ąÄ`, `‚ąÉ`, `‚Č•`, `őĶ`, `‚ĄĚ`, `‚Üí`, `‚Č†`, `‚ąß`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` ‚ÄĒ not verified in this review (requires build environment).
- [ ] Custom linters ‚ÄĒ not verified in this review (requires build environment).

## Summary

**PASS.** The file is well-structured and conforms to the checklist. The formalization models the ErdŇĎs‚ÄďBollob√°s conjecture (problem 578) by expressing the probabilistic containment of the hypercube Q_d in G(2^d, 1/2) as a counting statement over Boolean matrices. Two helper definitions (`hypercubeGraph`, `toGraph578`) are cleanly written with full proofs for their `SimpleGraph` fields and proper docstrings. The main theorem correctly uses Case 3 (direct assertion) without `answer()`, and is appropriately categorized as `research solved`. No issues found.

content.text: The review passed ‚ÄĒ no changes needed.
